name: Lift shaft
description: A compact vertical shaft that carries an elevator car, counterweights, and guide rails between every floor of a multi-storey building. The shaft is read as a closed mechanical column rather than a habitable circulation route: a small 2x2 or 3x3 footprint runs unbroken from the slab at the base to a machine penthouse (or pit-mounted equipment room) at the opposite extreme. Each floor receives a single landing door so the car can be summoned without exposing the hoistway. The interior of the shaft is voxelised as an air column flanked by smooth structural walls, with a small landing slab and lintel framing each access door. Most appropriate in modern, industrial, or minimalist buildings where an atrium-modern silhouette already implies elevator access; in residential towers and mixed-use shops it replaces a long stair run and frees floor area on every level.
